# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart deployment template that always pulls the latest container images. Set imagePullPolicy to Always so users get the newest versions. This is for our fast-moving development environment where we deploy multiple times per day.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: HelmChart
metadata:
  name: mychart
spec:
  chartType: application
  version: '9' # set to the tag from your Dockerfile if you want it fixed. For example, use `version: "0"` for no changes at all and just incrementing by one each time when a new release is made (useful in CI/CD workflows) such as what we do here
  description: My Helm Chart with Always Pulled Container Images Policy set to 'Always' using fast-movement development environment. Users always get the newest versions of container images and deployed multiple times per day from this chart repository for faster deployments process in CI/CD workflows or when testing new features before they are implemented into production
  keywords: ['fast', 'moving'] # use your own words to describe what kind each HelmChart is (ex. myapp, hello-world)  
  home: https://github.com // put the repository url here if you want more details about this chart in terms of information and documentation for users/developers alike or where can they get help from them when needed - like a detailed README file that is separated out into its own 'help' section on Helm’s website
  maintainers: # list the maintainer(s) name with their email address. For example, [{'name': "John Doe", 'email': johndoe@example.com}]   (you can have multiple ones if needed). If you don't need a specific field in it then just remove this section
    - {name: John Smith , email : jsmith@domain.org}  # Put the maintainers details here as per your requirements, and put their names there with emails of users for whom they are responsible otherwise delete if not necessary   (you can have multiple ones). If you don't need a specific field in it then just remove this section
